summary: The most common additional canal configuration involves both lateral extension into root bifurcations as well as presence within the furcation area for mandibular first molars; this reflects significant anatomic variation necessitating careful diagnostic imaging before procedures like endodontic therapy or restorations involving deep caries near apices where such variations could complicate outcomes if not accounted for properly through thorough pre-treatment evaluation using advanced radiographic techniques including CBCT scans when necessary. Key point here being awareness regarding complex internal tooth structures which may influence therapeutic decisions significantly impacting patient care quality positively by reducing procedural risks associated non-visualization due oversight leading potentially adverse consequences otherwise avoidable via meticulous planning based upon comprehensive assessment utilizing appropriate
